$comment: Machine-readable OneUI surface level taxonomy. 'attention' is the relative loudness; 'allowedLevel' is where it may appear; 'rarity' signals how often it is appropriate. Numeric step offsets reflect the engine's resolveSurface() relative to the parent step on the 25-step (100-2500) scale.

useFor: tiny nested separation inside a component
small info block where bold is too strong

bold.stepRule: brand base step (base if parent>=1300 else darkerBase); minimum 7-step jump with 700-offset fallback
bold.rule: at most one focal point per screen

ghost.stepRule: parent step (unchanged), still emits surface context
useFor: participating in the surface cascade without changing color

sparkle.stepRule: pinned sparkle/accent step (role, not numeric level)

roles.primary: actions, navigation, active states, main decisions, primary CTA
roles.secondary: non-interactive support, passive indicators (NOT a secondary button hierarchy)
roles.informative: info/system-status semantic; also drives focus rings

globalRules: Start every screen on default neutral.
A non-default surface needs a nameable purpose: guidance, promotion, interaction, separation, or elevation.
Solve hierarchy with spacing/type/layout before reaching for a surface.
One clear attention focal point per screen.
Text and strokes stay neutral unless link/action/nav/semantic/on-color-foreground.
Dark mode stays neutral — never tinted or brand-colored backgrounds.
Never hardcode background colors; always use <Surface mode> so adaptation works.
Color is computed automatically and is WCAG-safe — your only job is choosing the right level.
